rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in annex agree blocks within valid component type or implementation scopes based on the nature of the contract.
Use assume/guarantee syntax for component type contracts and assign for implementation-level bindings.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports and features only through visible identifiers in the AADL model.
Ensure all AGREE expressions use valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and match the actual types of referenced features.
Do not encode AADL property values directly into AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Generate stateful equations using initialized temporal recurrence and branching logic with nested if-then-else constructs.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Equation declarations use `eq name : type = expression;` and constant declarations use `const name : type = value;`.
Guarantees must be placed in component type scopes unless they refer to implementation-specific outputs.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing AADL property names like Period, Deadline, Compute_Execution_Time directly in AGREE expressions without model exposure.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for such cases.
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
